Name: keymgr.kmac_en_masking_i
Description: Indicates whether KMAC operates in masking mode; used by design assertions to ensure the integration’s masking configuration matches the key manager’s expectation.

Name: keymgr.key_vld
Description: Aggregate validity of the key inputs/selection for all operations.

Name: keymgr.u_cfgen.en_i
Description: The 'Configuration global enable input' (en_i) is crucial for controlling the configuration enable path. It qualifies initialization, participates in disabling (vld_dis), and directly gates the output (out_o). This ensures that configuration changes are only allowed when en_i is asserted, maintaining the integrity of the configuration process.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.en_i
Description: Global enable from lifecycle gates whether the controller accepts operations and influences state transitions and wiping.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.op_start_i
Description: Starts an operation (advance, generate, etc.) when asserted in conjunction with op_i.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.entropy_i
Description: Per-share PRNG entropy consumed to randomize/wipe key shares and for advance operations.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.sideload_sel_err_i
Description: Error with sideload destination selection.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.data_fsm_err
Description: Error from the data enable FSM controlling data_hw_en_o/data_sw_en_o.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.kmac_done_i
Description: Indicates completion of a KMAC operation to the op-state FSM.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.prng_reseed_done_i
Description: Indicates a reseed event completed (used to drive PRNG behavior after disable/invalid).

Name: keymgr_ctrl.prng_reseed_ack_i
Description: Acknowledges a reseed request to advance into randomization.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.op_done_o
Description: Indicates completion of an operation to software.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.data_valid_o
Description: Marks KMAC data valid phase during KeyUpdateKmac.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_data_en.hw_sel_i
Description: Selects which data path (hardware or software) is enabled when an id/gen operation is requested; invalid/ambiguous values cause the path to be disabled.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_data_en.adv_en_i
Description: Forces the FSM into the disable state during an advance operation and keeps data disabled until the completion sequence is observed.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_data_en.id_en_i
Description: Requests a non-advance operation that may enable data, steering to HwEn/SwEn based on the selector.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_data_en.op_done_i
Description: Transitions HwEn/SwEn/Dis to the Wait state where outputs are deasserted, shaping the completion sequence before returning to Idle.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_data_en.op_start_i
Description: Controls exit from the Wait state; only when deasserted does the FSM return to Idle to allow the next operation.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_data_en.fsm_err_o
Description: Asserted in the default case to indicate illegal/invalid FSM state detection (sparse-encoded FSM).

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_err.sideload_sel_err_i
Description: Indicates an illegal sideload key selection; contributes to synchronous fault collection (SyncFaultSideSel).

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_err.regfile_intg_err_i
Description: Signals integrity faults in the register file; contributes to AsyncFaultRegIntg and fault_o.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_err.ctrl_fsm_err_i
Description: Contributes to aggregated FSM integrity fault (AsyncFaultFsmIntg) that is propagated to fault_o.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_err.error_o
Description: Publishes final error codes gated by operation completion, summarizing synchronous and asynchronous errors.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_err.op_done_i
Description: Marks operation completion, clears latched sync error/fault accumulators, and gates the transfer of final status into error_o/fault_o.

Name: keymgr_input_checks.key_version_i
Description: Input key version candidate that is compared against the current maximum allowed version to decide acceptance.

Name: keymgr_input_checks.creator_seed_vld_o
Description: Indicates whether the creator seed passes the basic plausibility check.

Name: keymgr_input_checks.owner_seed_vld_o
Description: Indicates whether the owner seed passes the plausibility check.

Name: keymgr_input_checks.health_state_vld_o
Description: Indicates whether the health state input passes the plausibility check.

Name: keymgr_input_checks.key_version_vld_o
Description: Outputs whether the provided key version is within the allowed maximum.

Name: keymgr_input_checks.key_vld_o
Description: Aggregated validity flag across key shares indicating non-trivial key material.

Name: keymgr_kmac_if.adv_en_i
Description: One-hot command selector; when asserted it chooses adv_data_i, sets the number of rounds, and initiates the transaction.

Name: keymgr_kmac_if.id_en_i
Description: One-hot command selector to choose the ID generation path and its data for KMAC.

Name: keymgr_kmac_if.fsm_error_o
Description: Asserted when the sparse FSM enters an invalid state or when counter errors occur; indicates a terminal error condition.

Name: keymgr_kmac_if.kmac_error_o
Description: Captures kmac_data_i.error when KMAC signals done, propagating KMAC-side error status.

Name: keymgr_kmac_if.kmac_done_error_o
Description: Latched when KMAC asserts done outside the valid window (protocol violation).

Name: keymgr_kmac_if.cmd_error_o
Description: Flags one-hot violations and enable inconsistency across a transaction (one_hot_err_q | cmd_consty_err_q).

Name: keymgr_kmac_if.entropy_i
Description: Two-share entropy input used to formulate decoy_data and to populate data_o with decoys before completion.

Name: keymgr_kmac_if.done_o
Description: Indicates KMAC operation completion; used by control logic to acknowledge and return to idle after start de-asserts.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_op_state.kmac_done_i
Description: Signals completion of a KMAC operation and triggers state transitions, acknowledgments, or updates.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_op_state.op_ack_o
Description: Notifies upstream logic that the current operation is complete.

Name: keymgr_ctrl.u_op_state.op_fsm_err_o
Description: Flags an error when the FSM reaches an invalid/default state while allowing transaction completion.

Name: keymgr.u_reg.shadowed_storage_err_o
Description: Aggregates storage error conditions from all shadowed configuration fields and exposes them to the system as an alert/status output.

Name: keymgr.u_reg.shadowed_update_err_o
Description: Aggregates invalid shadowed write sequence errors for shadowed registers and exposes them to the system.

Name: keymgr.u_reg.intg_err_o
Description: Signals a detected integrity error on incoming TL-UL transactions or spurious write enables.

Name: keymgr.u_reseed_ctrl.reseed_req_i
Description: External control that requests an entropy reseed; when asserted and not already in progress, it causes the module to issue an EDN request.

Name: keymgr.u_reseed_ctrl.reseed_ack_o
Description: Provides an acknowledgement when the module has accepted a reseed request (coincident with EDN ack).

Name: keymgr.u_reseed_ctrl.cnt_err_o
Description: Indicates an error from the prim_count instance managing the reseed counter (e.g., redundancy check failure).

Name: keymgr_sideload_key_ctrl.u_aes_key.en_i
Description: Gates the valid output and also clears the internal valid state when low; valid_o = valid_q & en_i and valid_q is cleared when !en_i.

Name: keymgr_sideload_key_ctrl.u_aes_key.set_en_i
Description: On a set operation, selects whether key_q loads the provided key shares (key_i) or fills with entropy-derived data.

Name: keymgr_sideload_key_ctrl.u_aes_key.entropy_i
Description: Provides per-share entropy used to overwrite key storage on clear and to generate a dummy key when set_en_i is deasserted.

Name: keymgr_sideload_key_ctrl.u_aes_key.key_i
Description: Secret key shares provided to be stored into key_q when set_i is asserted with set_en_i enabled.

Name: keymgr_sideload_key_ctrl.u_aes_key.key_o
Description: Outputs the stored key shares for consumption by downstream cryptographic blocks.

Name: keymgr_sideload_key_ctrl.u_aes_key.valid_o
Description: Indicates to downstream logic whether the sideload key is valid and usable (valid_q AND en_i).

Name: keymgr_sideload_key_ctrl.entropy_i
Description: Entropy used by sideload key instances for masking/refresh during set and clear operations.

Name: keymgr_sideload_key_ctrl.sideload_sel_err_o
Description: Asserts if observed valids conflict with internal valid_tracking_q expectations, indicating an unexpected valid assertion.

Name: keymgr_sideload_key_ctrl.fsm_err_o
Description: Asserts on illegal/default FSM states in the sideload control.
